title: The Big Short
logline: A group of financial outsiders discover the impending collapse of the housing market and bet against the big banks, exposing the corruption and greed that led to the 2008 financial crisis.
protagonist: Multiple protagonists: Michael Burry, Mark Baum, Charlie Geller & Jamie Shipley
antagonistOrConflict: The corrupt financial system and housing bubble

actBreaks.description: All three storylines are established and the protagonists commit to betting against the housing market.

validation.ruling: Scene #42 introduces Jamie and Charlie working from their garage, establishing their backstory and investment philosophy while transitioning the narrative to include the third major protagonist group. This represents a structural shift that expands the story's scope beyond Michael Burry's initial setup.

actBreaks.description: The Las Vegas conference ends with protagonists realizing the full scope of the corruption and their bets' implications.

validation.ruling: Scene #111 shows Mark's confrontation with Mr. Chau reaching its climax as Mark calls him an idiot and demands half a billion more in swaps, despite the risk of bankruptcy. This represents the final major escalation before the collapse begins, fundamentally changing the stakes and Mark's commitment level.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script employs a sophisticated triptych structure, following three separate groups of financial outsiders who independently discover the same massive flaw in the system. Rather than a traditional single-protagonist arc, it weaves together Michael Burry's analytical discovery, Mark Baum's moral outrage, and Charlie/Jamie's opportunistic investigation. The structure mirrors the complexity of the financial crisis itself - multiple interconnected stories that converge toward the same catastrophic endpoint. The Vegas conference serves as a crucial convergence point where all storylines intersect, revealing the full scope of the systemic corruption.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after scene 42 when all protagonists are committed to their positions. The script uses rapid-fire montages and cross-cutting between storylines to build tension, particularly during the Miami investigation (scenes 52-74) and the Vegas conference (scenes 97-111). The final act slows to focus on the human cost and moral implications of being right about a disaster.

incitingIncident.description: Michael explains to Goldman Sachs representatives that he wants to buy credit default swaps on mortgage bonds, betting against the housing market, while they laugh at his seemingly foolish investment.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: 19 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - GOLDMAN SACHS - DAY 19 19

validation.ruling: Scene #12 is too early and lacks the irreversible commitment that defines an inciting incident. Scene #16 shows Michael meeting with Goldman Sachs to create credit default swaps against mortgage bonds - this is the moment he takes concrete action to short the housing market, establishing the main conflict and stakes that drive the entire story.

midpoint.description: Georgia Hale from Standard and Poors, wearing eye exam sunshades, dismisses Mark and Vinny's concerns about subprime bonds, insisting the banks care about their reputations and the marketplace is efficient.
midpoint.sceneHeading: 91 INT. SMALL OFFICE - RATING AGENCY SURVEILLANCE DEPT - DAY 91 91

validation.ruling: Scene #91 shows Mark and Vinny confronting Georgia at Standard & Poor's about rating agencies not downgrading subprime bonds, revealing the corrupt relationship between banks and rating agencies. This revelation shifts Mark from reactive investigation to proactive aggression, representing the point of no return where he fully commits to the bet against the system.

climax.description: Danny thinks he's having a heart attack from stress as Morgan Stanley loses a third of its value and the team can't track their 70 positions.
climax.sceneHeading: 143 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - LATER THAT DAY 143 143

validation.ruling: Scene #141 shows Charlie observing Lehman employees leaving, which is aftermath rather than confrontation. Scene #143 depicts Danny having a stress-induced heart attack as Morgan Stanley loses a third of its value and the team struggles to track their 70 positions - this represents the peak dramatic tension and highest stakes as the financial system collapses around them.

scenes.heading: 1 OPEN ON: INT. SOLOMON BROTHERS - 1979 - DAY 1 1
scenes.summary: Fat bond traders eat sandwiches and smoke on the Solomon Brothers trading floor while a narrator explains that banking was a boring, stable profession in the late seventies.

scenes.heading: 2 INT. SOLOMON BROTHERS - CONFERENCE ROOM - 1979 - DAY 2 2
scenes.summary: Lewis Ranieri presents mortgage-backed securities to pension fund managers, explaining how bundling thousands of mortgages together increases yield while bond traders make crude jokes.
scenes.turningPointNote: Introduction of mortgage-backed securities - the financial instrument that will drive the entire story

scenes.heading: 3 INT. STRIP CLUB - 1979 - NIGHT 3 3
scenes.summary: Bond traders party at a strip club throwing money around while the narrator explains how Lewis's team made their department the most profitable on Wall Street.

scenes.heading: 4 EXT. SKYLINE OF MANHATTAN - DUSK 4 4
scenes.summary: Banking buildings spring up across Manhattan's skyline over thirty years until 2008 when it all crashes down with Lehman Brothers declaring bankruptcy and foreclosure signs appearing everywhere.
scenes.turningPointNote: The crash of 2008 - the catastrophic outcome that frames the entire narrative

scenes.heading: 8 EXT. MIDDLE SCHOOL FOOTBALL FIELD - DAY 8 8
scenes.summary: Eleven-year-old Michael Burry catches a football and runs until he's tackled, his glass eye falls out on the field, and opposing players call him a freak.

scenes.heading: 9 INT. INDOOR POOL - DAY 9 9
scenes.summary: Adult Michael Burry swims laps alone and explains in voiceover how his glass eye makes social interactions awkward and keeps him comfortable in solitude.

scenes.heading: 10 INT. WATER FRONT RESTAURANT - NIGHT 10 10
scenes.summary: Michael stands alone at a party between groups of professionals while a lawyer brags about flipping a house for a $300,000 profit in two years.

scenes.heading: 11 EXT. WATER FRONT RESTAURANT - NIGHT 11 11
scenes.summary: Michael starts his car and leaves the party while explaining in voiceover that he went home and researched mortgage fraud rates until 3 AM.

scenes.heading: 12 INT. MICHAEL BURRY’S FUND OFFICE - DAY 12 12
scenes.summary: Michael tells a young analyst that mortgage fraud rates are at their highest since the 1930s, then awkwardly compliments the analyst's haircut during a job interview.

scenes.heading: 13 INT. SCION CAPITAL - MICHAEL BURRY’S OFFICE - NEXT DAY 13 13
scenes.summary: Michael reads thousands of complicated mortgage lines on his computer screen, analyzing foreclosures and adjustable rate mortgages while thinking about investment grade bonds full of high-risk mortgages.

scenes.heading: 14 INT. MICHAEL’S OFFICE - THE NEXT AFTERNOON 14 14
scenes.summary: Michael, disheveled and surrounded by water bottles, calls Lawrence Fields and tells him he wants to short the housing market instead of valuing stocks.
scenes.turningPointNote: Michael Burry decides to short the housing market - the central trade that drives his storyline

scenes.heading: 15 INT. METRO CAPITAL - LAWRENCE FIELDS’S OFFICE - SAME TIME 15 15
scenes.summary: Lawrence Fields laughs at Michael's idea from his lavish Manhattan office, citing Greenspan's assurance that bubbles are regional and defaults rare, while Michael insists Greenspan is wrong.

scenes.heading: 16 EXT. SMALL WATERFALL - DAY 16 16
scenes.summary: Scarlett Johansson explains by a waterfall how banks created private mortgage bonds without government standards, filling them with riskier mortgages to keep profits flowing.

scenes.heading: 17 INT. MICHAEL BURRY’S FUND OFFICE - CONTINUOUS 17 17
scenes.summary: Michael tells Lawrence he read the mortgage bond documents that no one else reads and believes the housing market is a time bomb he wants to short.

scenes.heading: 18 EXT. GOLDMAN SACHS - MANHATTAN - DAY 18 18
scenes.summary: Jim Cramer encourages investment on his TV show outside Goldman Sachs while a female sales rep asks Dr. Burry about his trade.

scenes.heading: 19 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - GOLDMAN SACHS - DAY 19 19
scenes.summary: Michael explains to Goldman Sachs representatives that he wants to buy credit default swaps on mortgage bonds, betting against the housing market, while they laugh at his seemingly foolish investment.

scenes.heading: 20 EXT. GOLDMAN SACHS HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 20 20
scenes.summary: Michael leaves the Goldman Sachs conference room and hears the sales reps laughing and clapping behind the closed door, then asks about buying $200 million in swaps.

scenes.heading: 21 INT. DEUTSCHE BANK - THE NEXT DAY 21 21
scenes.summary: Deutsche Bank sales reps agree to sell Michael the swaps he wants, and he pushes bundles of hand-picked mortgage bonds across tables at multiple banks for signing.

scenes.heading: 23 INT. FAST FOOD RESTAURANT - DAY 23 23
scenes.summary: Michael eats alone at a fast food restaurant when his phone buzzes with a photo from his wife and son saying "We miss you!"

scenes.heading: 24 INT. MANHATTAN NIGHT CLUB - NIGHT 24 24
scenes.summary: Deutsche Bank sales reps celebrate hard at a nightclub, toasting their $200 million credit swap sale to a California fund manager they think went crazy, while word spreads to other traders.

scenes.heading: 25 INT. MICHAEL BURRY’S FUND OFFICE - DAY 25 25
scenes.summary: Michael tells Lawrence Fields on the phone that he's invested $1.3 billion, prompting Lawrence to express distress that it's nearly all of Scion's liquidity.

scenes.heading: 26 INT. ACADEMY GALLERIA - FLORENCE 26 26
scenes.summary: Lawrence Fields argues with Michael from Florence, calling him a crazy man for the investment and threatening that they'll lose money paying premiums until mortgages fail, while Michael insists it's logical.

scenes.heading: 27 EXT. SUNNY ROOFTOP - DAY 27 27
scenes.summary: A cell phone lies on the ground while a voice calls out "Paul? Are you there Paul? Paul!"

scenes.heading: 28 INT. GROUP THERAPY SESSION - MANHATTAN - DAY 28 28
scenes.summary: Mark Baum bursts late into a group therapy session and immediately starts ranting about a retail banker who makes billions screwing customers with overdraft penalties instead of discussing his personal issues.

scenes.heading: 29 EXT. MANHATTAN STREETS - LATER 29 29
scenes.summary: Mark walks Manhattan streets past luxury billboards while talking on his phone about refusing bad money business, as the narrator introduces him as someone with a nose for bullshit.

scenes.heading: INT. YASHIVA SCHOOL - DAY A30
scenes.summary: Young Mark Baum studies Torah intensely at Yashiva school while the Rabbi tells his mother that Mark searches for inconsistencies in God's word, and the narrator explains how tragedy later darkened Mark's worldview.

scenes.heading: 30 INT. APARTMENT LIBRARY - PARK AVENUE - NEW YORK - DAY 30 30
scenes.summary: Mark's wife Cynthia confronts him about missing therapy again while their children play nearby, and Mark rants about the financial shit storm and how average people walk around oblivious to being screwed.

scenes.heading: 31 EXT. MANHATTAN STREETS - CONTINUOUS 31 31
scenes.summary: People walk in slow motion down Manhattan streets while Mark continues his rant about credit card fees and predatory lending, and Cynthia suggests he try medication and quit Wall Street.

scenes.heading: 33 INT. APARTMENT LIBRARY - PARK AVENUE 33 33
scenes.summary: Cynthia tells Mark she knows how close he was to his brother and hopes he'll talk about what happened, noting how distrustful he's become since the tragedy.

scenes.heading: 34 EXT. MANHATTAN STREETS 34 34
scenes.summary: Mark hails a cab while Cynthia suggests they move to Vermont and open a B&B to get off the treadmill, and Mark reluctantly agrees to consider it.

scenes.heading: 35 EXT. MORGAN STANLEY BUILDING - LATER 35 35
scenes.summary: Mark gets out of a cab at Morgan Stanley, warns his driver about mutual fund fees, and greets Kathy Tao who mentions she's expecting while he explains his afternoon absence.

scenes.heading: 36 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - MORGAN STANLEY NEW YORK - LATER 36 36
scenes.summary: Mark enters his small FrontPoint office and tells his team that Cynthia wants him to quit and open a B&B, while the narrator introduces his team members and their backgrounds of distrust toward the system.

scenes.heading: 37 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - THE NEXT DAY 37 37
scenes.summary: Jared Vennett and his team present to Mark's group while Mark asks how many people Jared has talked to about the trade, followed by quick cuts of people rejecting Jared's pitch.

scenes.heading: 39 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS 39 39
scenes.summary: Jared uses a Jenga game to explain mortgage bond tranches and how B and BB level tranches became worthless dog shit, while Mark's team listens to his pitch about shorting the housing market.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jared's explanation of how mortgage bonds became toxic - key exposition that drives Mark's team toward the trade

scenes.heading: 40 INT. CHEF’S KITCHEN - DAY 40 40
scenes.summary: Anthony Bourdain explains in a kitchen how old fish gets chopped up and reused in fish stew, comparing it to how bad mortgages get repackaged into CDOs.

scenes.heading: 41 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - CONTINUOUS 41 41
scenes.summary: Mark realizes CDOs are dog shit wrapped in garbage while Jared explains that institutions treat them like treasury bonds, and Danny expresses disbelief that banks and government would allow this to happen.

scenes.heading: 42 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - DAY 42 42
scenes.summary: Mark's team clusters unhappily at their desks suspecting Jared is playing them, while Mark argues that banks have trained people to trust them with money and financing is just a fancy term for getting raped.

scenes.heading: 43 INT. HIGH END DANCE CLUB - NIGHT 43 43
scenes.summary: Jamie Shipley and Charlie Geller dance badly at a high-end Chelsea club, chasing away women with their awkward approaches and direct attempts at conversation.

scenes.heading: 44 INT. LOBBY WAITING AREA - JP MORGAN - NEW YORK - DAY 44 44
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie nervously wait at JP Morgan under massive art pieces until a young banker tells them they can't find any marketing material on their fund and questions their legitimacy.

scenes.heading: 45 OMIT 45 45
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 46 OMIT 46 46
scenes.summary: Scene omitted.

scenes.heading: 47 EXT. BOULDER COLORADO - GARAGE - SUMMER DAY 47 47
scenes.summary: Jamie and Charlie work on laptops in a hot garage with computer paper reading "The Brownfield Fund" while the narrator explains their strategy of betting on unlikely bad events that people underestimate.

scenes.heading: 48 INT. JP MORGAN LOBBY - CONTINUOUS 48 48
scenes.summary: Jamie tells Charlie they're dead in the water without an ISDA from any bank, and Charlie suggests they call Ben.

scenes.heading: 49 EXT. RESIDENTIAL STREET - DAY 49 49
scenes.summary: The narrator introduces Ben Rickert as a former Singapore trader who quit in disgust and became Jamie's neighbor, describing him as dark and believing the whole world was going down.

scenes.heading: 50 INT. SAM’S HOUSE - NIGHT 50 50
scenes.summary: Jamie and Charlie visit Ben's house filled with water jugs while Ben rants about declining precipitation and Congress cutting budgets for loose nukes, as the narrator explains Ben's paranoid preparedness.

scenes.heading: 51 INT. JP MORGAN LOBBY - CONTINUOUS 51 51
scenes.summary: Jamie suggests doing research before calling Ben while Charlie reads the shocking pitch, and a security guard demands they leave the JP Morgan lobby.

scenes.heading: 52 INT. TERMINAL - MIAMI AIRPORT - DAY 52 52
scenes.summary: Porter and Danny walk through Miami airport past Dolphins merchandise while Porter complains about Mark sending him on another fact-finding mission, this time to Florida.

scenes.heading: 53 EXT. UNFINISHED SUBDIVISION - DUSK 53 53
scenes.summary: Porter and Danny examine a failed housing development with four model houses sitting alone behind cyclone fencing as street lights come on across the empty subdivision.

scenes.heading: 54 INT. CONFERENCE HALL - CORPORATE HOTEL - THAT NIGHT 54 54
scenes.summary: Two hundred people attend a "Real Estate Millionaire" seminar where the leader claims real estate requires no capital while Porter looks stunned and a greeter tries to sell them a $2999 CD set.

scenes.heading: 55 EXT. FRONT PORCH - RANCH HOUSE - NEXT DAY 55 55
scenes.summary: Danny knocks on the door of an empty ranch house and looks through the mail slot to confirm no one lives there.

scenes.heading: 56 EXT. FRONT PORCH - ANOTHER RANCH HOUSE - DAY 56 56
scenes.summary: Porter checks newspapers on a porch and finds they are three months old.

scenes.heading: 57 EXT. FRONT PORCH - YET ANOTHER RANCH HOUSE - DAY 57 57
scenes.summary: Danny surveys a delinquent homeowner but discovers the mortgage was taken out using the landlord's dog's name, and the tenant fears eviction despite paying rent.

scenes.heading: 58 INT. FOYER - STILL YET ANOTHER RANCH HOUSE - DAY 58 58
scenes.summary: Danny and Porter peer into an abandoned house with dusty furnishings.

scenes.heading: 59 INT. LIVING ROOM AND HOME OFFICE - CONTINUOUS 59 59
scenes.summary: Porter finds dead lights and scattered files in the abandoned house, while Danny calls him over after discovering something.

scenes.heading: 60 INT. DEN AND KITCHEN - CONTINUOUS 60 60
scenes.summary: Danny shows Porter a mortgage bill where the owner circled the amount due and wrote 'SORRY!' in black marker.

scenes.heading: 61 EXT. BACK YARD - CONTINUOUS 61 61
scenes.summary: Porter and Danny discover an overgrown backyard with a murky pool containing a 10-foot alligator.

scenes.heading: 62 EXT. CUBAN RESTAURANT - DAY 62 62
scenes.summary: Porter calls Mark from outside a Cuban restaurant to tell him he should come down to Florida.

scenes.heading: 63 INT. SCION OFFICE - DAY 63 63
scenes.summary: Scion's office shows empty desks and negative returns while analysts gossip about Burry having a breakdown, and his assistant fields calls saying he prefers email.

scenes.heading: 64 INT. SCION OFFICES - MIKE'S OFFICE - DAY 64 64
scenes.summary: Lawrence and Martin confront Mike about his mortgage bet, questioning his ability to identify macroeconomic trends, while Mike argues that obvious bubble markers exist.

scenes.heading: 65 INT. MERCEDES SUV - UPSCALE SUBDIVISION - DAY 65 65
scenes.summary: Mark tours an upscale subdivision with real estate agents, observing wealthy homeowners with expensive possessions while the agent describes the market as being in a small downturn.

scenes.heading: 66 EXT. TRACT MANSION - DAY 66 66
scenes.summary: The real estate agent points out an attractive house that has appreciated significantly in value over multiple sales.

scenes.heading: 67 I/E. MERCEDES SUV - DAY 67 67
scenes.summary: The real estate agent explains the house's price history and reveals the current owners are selling because neither is working, though she waves off showing it today.

scenes.heading: 68 I/E. SUV - TRAVELLING - DAY 68 68
scenes.summary: The SUV drives past multiple houses for sale as the agent describes various sellers as 'motivated' in different degrees.

scenes.heading: 69 OMIT 69 69
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 70 INT. HOTEL BAR - SOUTH BEACH - DAY 70 70
scenes.summary: Mark, Danny, and Porter interview two mortgage brokers who reveal they've increased their loan volume from 10-15 to 60 per month, with one formerly being a bartender.

scenes.heading: 71 INT. HOTEL LOBBY - SECONDS LATER 71 71
scenes.summary: Mark whispers with Porter and Danny about the brokers, realizing they're not confessing but bragging about their practices.

scenes.heading: 72 INT. HOTEL BAR - DAY 72 72
scenes.summary: The mortgage brokers explain their targeting strategies - one focuses on immigrants who don't ask questions, while the other targets subprime borrowers for higher commissions.

scenes.heading: 73 INT. ALCOVE - VIP ROOM - STRIP CLUB - NIGHT 73 73
scenes.summary: A dancer at a strip club explains her mortgage strategy to Mark during a lap dance, revealing she has multiple loans on properties and lies about her profession on applications.

scenes.heading: 74 INT. TICKETING AREA - MIAMI AIRPORT - DAY 74 74
scenes.summary: Mark strides through Miami airport instructing his team to buy mortgage swaps while declaring it's time to call bullshit on everything.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark commits to betting against the housing market after witnessing the fraud firsthand

scenes.heading: 75 INT. GYM - DAY 75 75
scenes.summary: Jared takes a call from Vinny at the gym, stepping off his treadmill to continue the conversation.

scenes.heading: 76 INT. TRADING DESK - FRONTPOINT OFFICE - DAY 76 76
scenes.summary: Vinny asks Jared how he plans to screw them over in the swap deal.

scenes.heading: 77 INT. GYM - DAY 77 77
scenes.summary: Jared explains he'll manipulate swap prices in the dark market and rip them off later, but they'll make so much money they won't care, then celebrates when Vinny agrees to the deal.

scenes.heading: 78 OMIT 78 78
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: EXT. WIDE SHOT OF BOULDER COLORADO - DAY A79
scenes.summary: Stock footage shows Boulder, Colorado with a phone ringing.

scenes.heading: INT. BEN RICKERT’S HOME OFFICE - BOULDER COLORADO - DAY B79
scenes.summary: Ben Rickert answers Jamie's call in his home office filled with multiple computer screens, hanging up when Jamie uses the wrong phone but answering the satellite phone.

scenes.heading: 79 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - GREENWICH VILLAGE - DAY 79 79
scenes.summary: Jamie and Charlie call Ben from their brick loft office, with Ben admitting the mortgage situation scared him and asking about CDOs.

scenes.heading: INT. KICHEN -- DAY A80
scenes.summary: Bourdain explains that CDOs are even worse than previously described, filled with various types of garbage investments beyond just bad mortgages.

scenes.heading: INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - GREENWICH VILLAGE - DAY B80
scenes.summary: Ben tells Charlie and Jamie the CDOs are worthless crap, and they explain they've shorted various tranches with 25-to-1 payoffs, asking Ben to help them get an ISDA agreement.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY AIRPORT TERMINAL - DAY C80
scenes.summary: Ben arrives at Kennedy Airport wearing a flu mask, salutes another masked passenger, and walks past Charlie and Jamie's sign without stopping.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEUTSCHE BANK - TWO HOURS LATER D80
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Deutsche Bank building.

scenes.heading: 80 INT. LOBBY - DEUTSCHE BANK - DAY 80 80
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie sign an ISDA contract in Deutsche Bank's lobby with Noah, then Ben explains they now own 12 million dollars of credit default swaps and tells them to wait for the world to blow up.
scenes.turningPointNote: Charlie and Jamie officially enter the big short with their ISDA contract

scenes.heading: 82 EXT. MIDTOWN - MORGAN STANLEY BUILDING - DAY 82 82
scenes.summary: Mark runs down the street calling Vinny while news reports show mortgage delinquencies hitting new highs, then gets in a cab heading to Morgan Stanley.

scenes.heading: 83 INT. FRONTPOINT PARTNERS OFFICE - DAY 83 83
scenes.summary: Vinny explains to Mark over the phone that despite mortgage defaults rising, subprime bond prices are up and Vennett is demanding more collateral on their swaps.

scenes.heading: 84 INT. CAB - SAME TIME 84 84
scenes.summary: Mark curses in the cab while the West African driver agrees with his assessment of American corruption and greed.

scenes.heading: 85 INT. TINY SIDE OFFICE - FRONTPOINT - DAY 85 85
scenes.summary: Vinny tells Mark that FrontPoint risk managers and pregnant Kathy Tao are trying to force them to sell their swaps, questioning if this is one of Mark's crusades.

scenes.heading: 86 INT. CAB - DAY 86 86
scenes.summary: Mark instructs Vinny to tell the risk assessors to fuck off and meet him at Standard and Poors, while the cab driver compares American financial theft to child soldiers in his country.

scenes.heading: 87 INT. CONFERENCE ROOM - FRONTPOINT PARTNERS - DAY 87 87
scenes.summary: Vinny enters the conference room and calmly tells the risk managers that Mark said to fuck off.

scenes.heading: 88 INT. SCION OFFICES - DAY 88 88
scenes.summary: Michael Burry writes worse quarterly returns on the whiteboard in his nearly empty office, then closes his door and screams in frustration.

scenes.heading: 89 INT. MICHAEL BURRY’S HOME - LIVING ROOM - DUSK 89 89
scenes.summary: Michael watches business news at home while his wife tries to discuss their son Nicholas having issues at school, but he's distracted by the TV coverage dismissing mortgage problems.

scenes.heading: 90 OMIT 90 90
scenes.summary: This scene is omitted.

scenes.heading: 91 INT. SMALL OFFICE - RATING AGENCY SURVEILLANCE DEPT - DAY 91 91
scenes.summary: Georgia Hale from Standard and Poors, wearing eye exam sunshades, dismisses Mark and Vinny's concerns about subprime bonds, insisting the banks care about their reputations and the marketplace is efficient.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark discovers the rating agencies are willfully blind to the crisis, revealing the depth of systemic corruption

scenes.heading: 92 INT. CAB - DAY 92 92
scenes.summary: Mark, rattled by the meeting, tells Vinny to short all the rating agencies while riding in a cab.

scenes.heading: 93 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - NYC LOFT - DAY 93 93
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie express frustration to Ben over the phone about mortgage delinquencies rising while CDO values increase, calling it completely backwards.

scenes.heading: INT. BEN RICKERT’S OFFICE - SAME TIME A94
scenes.summary: Ben reveals his Bear contact doesn't know what CDOs are, while Charlie realizes the banks might be guessing at CDO values and suggests buying more swaps despite Jamie's concerns about losing money.

scenes.heading: 94 INT. FRONTPOINT PARTNERS - SAME TIME 94 94
scenes.summary: Mark's team screams at Jared Vennett for screwing them over with higher bond prices despite rising defaults, while Jared calmly checks his phone and waits for them to finish yelling.

scenes.heading: 95 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - NYC LOFT - SAME TIME 95 95
scenes.summary: Charlie pleads to go all in on their bet, wanting to know definitively if they're right or wrong, while Jamie lacks confidence and Ben suggests going to Vegas.

scenes.heading: 96 INT. FRONTPOINT PARTNERS - SAME TIME 96 96
scenes.summary: Jared suggests they attend the American Securitization Forum in Vegas to meet the 'dumb money' they're betting against, while Porter complains about hating Vegas.

scenes.heading: 97 INT. VENETIAN HOTEL - STATUES - DAY 97 97
scenes.summary: Living statues at the Venetian Hotel move and look into the camera as hip hop music begins.

scenes.heading: 98 INT. MAIN CASINO FLOOR OF CAESAR’S PALACE - DAY 98 98
scenes.summary: Quick images show cash being exchanged for chips, a waitress with a baby tattoo, a Marine gambling, and a poster of happy gamblers at Caesar's Palace.

scenes.heading: 99 INT. TRADE SHOW FLOOR - CAESAR’S PALACE - DAY 99 99
scenes.summary: Ben gives Charlie and Jamie a tour of the American Securitization Forum trade show, testing their knowledge of financial terms and explaining their goal to determine if their deal is legitimate or if they're about to be screwed.
scenes.turningPointNote: The protagonists enter the belly of the beast to investigate their bet at the industry's main gathering

scenes.heading: 100 INT. SHOOTING RANGE - THE GUN STORE - LAS VEGAS - DAY 100 100
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie shoot guns at a Las Vegas shooting range with Bear Stearns representatives who mock their company name and refuse to provide insight on mortgage bonds.

scenes.heading: 101 INT. GIANT BALLROOM - SAME TIME 101 101
scenes.summary: Mark and his team attend a mortgage industry conference where Option One's CEO speaks about the strong mortgage market despite the company's recent subprime losses.

scenes.heading: 102 INT. SCION OFFICE - SAN JOSE - DAY 102 102
scenes.summary: Mike Burry reads emails from angry investors withdrawing money from his fund as his office empties to just three employees and quarterly returns show -19.3%.

scenes.heading: INT. BURRY’S BASEMENT - LATER A103
scenes.summary: Mike furiously plays heavy metal drums in his basement until he breaks the drumsticks and screams in frustration.

scenes.heading: 103 INT. TRADE SHOW FLOOR - LATER 103 103
scenes.summary: Charlie and Ben negotiate with a Merrill Lynch representative who demands 5% premiums for BBB swaps, up from 2% two months earlier.

scenes.heading: 104 EXT. CASINO HOTEL - POOL SIDE - SAME TIME 104 104
scenes.summary: Jamie asks his SEC friend Evie about mortgage bond investigations and learns the SEC doesn't investigate mortgage bonds due to budget cuts.

scenes.heading: 105 INT. CASINO RESTAURANT - LUNCH 105 105
scenes.summary: Charlie proposes betting against AA-rated CDO tranches as a cheaper alternative, and they successfully negotiate a deal with Deutsche Bank for $200 million in swaps.
scenes.turningPointNote: Charlie and Jamie secure their major bet against the economy through AA-rated CDO swaps

scenes.heading: 107 INT. CASINO - DAY 107 107
scenes.summary: Ben confronts Charlie and Jamie about betting against the American economy, explaining that unemployment increases cause deaths and affect real people's lives.

scenes.heading: 108 INT. TABEMASU ASIAN RESTAURANT - HOTEL - NIGHT 108 108
scenes.summary: Mark meets CDO manager Mr. Chau at a Japanese restaurant where Chau explains how he selects securities for CDO portfolios and reveals his massive betting operation.

scenes.heading: 109 INT. CASINO FLOOR - NIGHT 109 109
scenes.summary: Selena Gomez and Dr. Thaler explain synthetic CDOs using a blackjack analogy, showing how side bets multiplied the housing market's risk exponentially.

scenes.heading: 110 INT. TABEMASU ASIAN RESTAURANT - CONTINUOUS 110 110
scenes.summary: Mark tells Mr. Chau he thinks he's an idiot rather than a parasite and walks away from their dinner meeting.

scenes.heading: 111 EXT. CAESAR’S PALACE - THE NEXT MORNING 111 111
scenes.summary: Hungover conference attendees leave Caesar's Palace in the morning sun, including Charlie and Jamie looking ashamed and Mr. Chau smiling confidently.

scenes.heading: 112 EXT. UPSCALE CLOTHING BOUTIQUE - "EDIT NEW YORK" - DAY 112 112
scenes.summary: Mark struggles to get his bag from a taxi outside his wife's clothing boutique.

scenes.heading: 113 INT. UPSCALE CLOTHING BOUTIQUE - DAY 113 113
scenes.summary: Mark tells his wife Cynthia that the economy might collapse but they could make half a billion dollars, leading to a conversation about his deceased brother Paul.

scenes.heading: 114 INT. WAITING AREA - PEDIATRIC PSYCH CENTER - DUSK 114 114
scenes.summary: Mike arrives late to pick up his son Nicholas from a pediatric psychology center where his wife reveals their son has been diagnosed with Asperger's Syndrome.

scenes.heading: 115 INT. LIVING ROOM - BURRY HOUSE - NIGHT 115 115
scenes.summary: Mike reads books about Asperger's Syndrome and recognizes the symptoms in himself through various text passages about autism spectrum behaviors.

scenes.heading: 116 INT. HOSPITAL ROOF - SAN JOSE - DAY 116 116
scenes.summary: Mike's med school friend on a hospital roof explains that an adult Asperger's diagnosis is positive because it explains his unique ability to read bond prospectuses.

scenes.heading: 117 INT. SCION - MIKE'S OFFICE - DAY 117 117
scenes.summary: Mike types and sends an email to investors restricting withdrawals from his fund, citing a fraudulent mortgage bond market, then sits alone in his empty office.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mike locks in his investors by restricting withdrawals, fully committing to his position

scenes.heading: 119 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - NYC LOFT - DAY 119 119
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie watch financial news and spot a crawl announcing New Century Financial's bankruptcy, marking the beginning of the crash on April 2, 2007.
scenes.turningPointNote: The housing market crash officially begins with New Century Financial's bankruptcy

scenes.heading: 120 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - DAY 120 120
scenes.summary: Mark's team watches Fed Chairman Bernanke downplay subprime troubles on TV while the Dow drops 283 points and Vinny orders all financial news turned off.

scenes.heading: 121 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - NYC LOFT - SAME TIME 121 121
scenes.summary: Charlie calls his mother about the global economic tsunami while Jamie shows him an email from Bear Stearns claiming CDOs haven't moved despite clear market indicators.

scenes.heading: 122 INT. NEWS ROOM - WALL STREET JOURNAL - DAY 122 122
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie try to convince Wall Street Journal reporter Casey to write about the banking fraud, but he refuses to risk his Wall Street relationships.

scenes.heading: 123 EXT. HALLWAY - WALL STREET JOURNAL - CONTINUOUS 123 123
scenes.summary: Jamie realizes that if Bear Stearns owns tons of CDOs and goes under, they could lose everything since they bought 80% of their swaps from Bear.

scenes.heading: 124 INT. SCION OFFICES - DAY 124 124
scenes.summary: Mike sits alone in his emptied office as lawyers remove files while he tries unsuccessfully to reach Lucy Thalia at Goldman Sachs about his positions.

scenes.heading: 125 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - DAY 125 125
scenes.summary: Mark's trading desk buzzes with activity as Countrywide Financial warns of difficult conditions and the Dow drops 325 points while Mark takes calls.

scenes.heading: 126 INT. DUETSCHE BATHROOM - DEUTSCHE BANK - DAY 126 126
scenes.summary: Jared calls Mark from a Deutsche Bank bathroom to report that swaps are now the most valuable product and warns that Morgan Stanley might be taking on water.

scenes.heading: 127 INT. MORGAN STANLEY HALLWAY - MOMENTS LATER 127 127
scenes.summary: Mark strides through Morgan Stanley's hallway past a crying woman carrying a box of personal effects after being fired.

scenes.heading: 128 INT. MORGAN STANLEY - KATHY TAO’S OFFICE - DAY 128 128
scenes.summary: Kathy Tao reveals that Morgan Stanley's Bennie Leibman lost money shorting subprime but then bought CDOs to cover premiums, creating massive exposure for the bank.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mark discovers Morgan Stanley's catastrophic exposure through their own trader's failed hedging strategy

scenes.heading: 129 INT. MANHATTAN RESTAURANT - LATE NIGHT 129 129
scenes.summary: Mark's team realizes they could lose everything even if they're right because Morgan Stanley's failure would put their accounts on the bank's balance sheet.

scenes.heading: 130 EXT. STREETS OF CHELSEA - DAY 130 130
scenes.summary: Jamie walks down a Chelsea street on his phone discussing Bear Stearns' mortgage hedge fund collapse and class action lawsuit on August 3, 2007.

scenes.heading: 131 INT. SPIN CLASS - SAME TIME 131 131
scenes.summary: Charlie yells over loud spin class music to Jamie about Bear Stearns potentially collapsing and realizes they don't know how to sell their swaps.

scenes.heading: 132 INT. SCION - MIKE'S OFFICE - SAN JOSE - DAY 132 132
scenes.summary: Mike calls to unwind his $1.3 billion position as default rates exceed 12% and the Dow drops 420 points on January 14, 2008.

scenes.heading: 133 INT. SCION OFFICE - NIGHT 133 133
scenes.summary: Mike erases the -19.3% from his whiteboard and starts to write positive returns before stopping to look at his empty office full of silent phones.

scenes.heading: 134 EXT. THE POWDER MONKEY PUB - EXMOUTH, ENGLAND - DAY 134 134
scenes.summary: Ben sets up at an English pub with poor reception to sell $200 million worth of securities while on vacation.

scenes.heading: 135 INT. POWDER MONKEY PUB - EXMOUTH, ENGLAND - DAY 135 135
scenes.summary: Ben conducts financial calls from the pub while local patrons listen to him trying to sell credit default swaps worth millions.

scenes.heading: 136 INT. BROWNFIELD OFFICE - EARLY MORNING 136 136
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie celebrate selling their positions for $80 million through Ben's efforts as European banks begin freezing customer accounts.

scenes.heading: 137 INT. AUDITORIUM - DEUTSCHE BANK HQ - DAY 137 137
scenes.summary: Mark debates bullish investor Bruce Miller at a Deutsche Bank conference where Miller defends Bear Stearns stock just before the bank's collapse.

scenes.heading: 138 INT. BACK HALLWAY TO AUDITORIUM - DAY 138 138
scenes.summary: A PR person leads Alan Greenspan and his entourage toward the auditorium for his scheduled speech.

scenes.heading: 139 INT. AUDITORIUM - DAY 139 139
scenes.summary: Greenspan arrives to find only six people remaining in the vast auditorium after Mark's devastating debate performance.

scenes.heading: 140 EXT. TIMES SQUARE - PRE-DAWN 140 140
scenes.summary: Times Square's electronic scroll flickers back to life displaying news of Lehman's bankruptcy leaving the country in turmoil.

scenes.heading: 141 EXT. PLAZA - LEHMAN BROTHERS HQ - DAY 141 141
scenes.summary: Charlie watches Lehman employees exit with boxes through a press gauntlet while Jamie reports the Dow has dropped 1800 points in three days.
scenes.turningPointNote: Lehman Brothers collapses, marking the peak of the financial crisis

scenes.heading: 142 EXT. WEST 49TH STREET - DAY 142 142
scenes.summary: A departing Lehman employee gives Charlie and Jamie his ID badge to enter the building and tells them which floor houses fixed income.

scenes.heading: 143 INT. FRONTPOINT OFFICE - LATER THAT DAY 143 143
scenes.summary: Danny thinks he's having a heart attack from stress as Morgan Stanley loses a third of its value and the team can't track their 70 positions.

scenes.heading: 144 INT. LEHMAN ELEVATOR BANKS - DAY 144 144
scenes.summary: A Lehman executive directs departing employees to avoid the press while Jamie and Charlie use the borrowed ID to enter the building.

scenes.heading: 145 INT. TRADING FLOOR - LEHMAN BROTHERS - DAY 145 145
scenes.summary: Charlie and Jamie walk through Lehman's abandoned trading floor littered with personal effects and a painted sign showing the stock price at zero.

scenes.heading: 146 EXT. ST. PATRICK'S CATHEDRAL - DAY 146 146
scenes.summary: Danny recovers from his panic attack on the steps of St. Patrick's Cathedral while Vinny finally reaches Mark by phone.

scenes.heading: 147 EXT. UPPER EAST SIDE APARTMENT ROOF GARDEN - SAME TIME 147 147
scenes.summary: Mark sits shell-shocked on his apartment roof garden as Vinny reports Morgan's stock has lost half its value and clients want to withdraw money.

scenes.heading: 148 INT. SCION OFFICE - DAY 148 148
scenes.summary: Mike sorts through mail at his assistant's desk in his empty office while listening to voicemails and reflecting on how making money kills essential parts of life.

scenes.heading: 149 INT. MINI-MART - DAY 149 149
scenes.summary: Burry's former assistant stocks Red Bull cans in a convenience store.

scenes.heading: EXT. UPSCALE MIAMI NEIGHBORHOOD - DAY A150
scenes.summary: The upscale Miami neighborhood that Mark toured with the real estate agent is now covered with foreclosure signs.

scenes.heading: 150 INT. SEMINAR ROOM - CORPORATE HOTEL - DAY 150 150
scenes.summary: Marlene and the Younger Agent attend a real estate seminar where the leader promotes the housing crisis as the greatest financial opportunity of their lifetime.

scenes.heading: 151 EXT. CONVENIENCE STORE - DAY 151 151
scenes.summary: The Man with the Tattooed Head returns to his car carrying food, revealing his family now lives in their vehicle, while Mike's voiceover describes his internal anguish and professional isolation.

scenes.heading: 152 INT. BASKETBALL ARENA - MIAMI UNIVERSITY - DAY 152 152
scenes.summary: Maroon waits in line at a university job fair while Mike's voiceover reflects on how people choose authority based on familiarity rather than facts or results.

scenes.heading: 153 INT. CAB - DAY 153 153
scenes.summary: The West African Cab Driver examines his 401K statement showing a loss of over $43,000, leaving him with only $46,000.

scenes.heading: 154 INT. MIKE'S OFFICE - NIGHT 154 154
scenes.summary: Mike types an email announcing his decision to close down the fund while his voiceover delivers the sullen realization.
scenes.turningPointNote: Mike makes the definitive decision to shut down his successful fund, marking the end of his involvement in the crisis

scenes.heading: 155 INT. SCION OFFICE - NIGHT 155 155
scenes.summary: Mike writes his fund's growth of +489% on the whiteboard, circles it, and walks out of the empty Scion office for the final time.

scenes.heading: 156 INT. OFFICE - DEUTSCHE BANK - DAY 156 156
scenes.summary: Jared Vennett addresses the camera about his $47 million bonus, defending himself by noting his bosses lost $30 billion but still received $50 million bonuses.

scenes.heading: 157 EXT. UPPER EAST SIDE APARTMENT ROOF GARDEN - DAY 157 157
scenes.summary: Mark tells Vinny over the phone that Paulson and Bernanke left the White House and there will be a bailout, realizing the banks knew taxpayers would rescue them and predicting future scapegoating of immigrants.
scenes.turningPointNote: The bailout confirmation reveals the full scope of the systemic corruption and Mark's devastating realization about the rigged system

scenes.heading: 160 EXT. UPPER EAST SIDE APARTMENT ROOF GARDEN - CONTINUOUS 160 160
scenes.summary: Mark agrees to sell all positions for nearly a billion dollars in profit after Vinny convinces him they're different from the predatory banks, followed by text revealing the massive financial losses from the collapse.
scenes.turningPointNote: The final decision to cash out completes the protagonists' journey while the statistics reveal the devastating human cost of the crisis

structuralWarnings.text: 2 of 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
